fields.summary: Automate first-pass claims adjudication with a rules engine
fields.description:
Adjusters are manually reviewing every incoming claim, including the routine ones. We want the system to auto-approve clean claims so that adjusters only spend time on exceptions and fraud.

Acceptance Criteria

  • A clean claim is auto-adjudicated in under 2 seconds
  • Every decision writes a full audit trail of which rules fired
  • Claims that look fraudulent are flagged, never auto-paid

Given a claim that passes all validation rules
When the adjudication engine evaluates it
Then the claim is auto-approved and the payout is scheduled

The engine must comply with state prompt-pay regulations. See CLM-4300 for the rule catalog.

comments.body: For the rules, let's use Drools. Business analysts can edit the DRL rules without waiting for a deploy.
We considered hardcoding the rules in Java but rejected it — every rate change would need a release. We decided to use Kafka to stream claim events into the engine so we can replay them.

A real performance risk: complex multi-line claims could blow the 2s SLA under peak load. We should cap rule-set complexity per claim type.

This is blocked by ACT-22, the actuarial rating-table service — without it we cannot compute allowed amounts.

Reproduced this in staging:

2026-06-20 09:14:33 ERROR AdjudicationEngine - rule evaluation failed for claim c-33219
java.lang.NumberFormatException: For input string: "N/A"
	at java.base/java.lang.Long.parseLong(Long.java:711)
	at io.claims.rules.ClaimFacts.getAllowedAmount(ClaimFacts.java:52)
	at org.drools.core.rule.Declaration.getValue(Declaration.java:239)
	at io.claims.rules.AdjudicationEngine.evaluate(AdjudicationEngine.java:88)
	at io.claims.rules.AdjudicationEngine.run(AdjudicationEngine.java:51)
2026-06-20 09:14:33 WARN AdjudicationEngine - routing claim c-33219 to manual review

That's a malformed test claim with a missing amount. TODO: add JSON-schema validation on the claim intake endpoint so garbage never reaches the engine.

We still need to add the fraud-model feature flag before we enable auto-approve in production.
